risk_highlights.loss_history_summary: Over the 5-year review period, the insured incurred 48 total claims with $1,378,733.13 in total paid losses and $1,510,580.11 total incurred. Currently 13 claims remain open and 35 have been closed. Annualized loss ratio is 12.9% based on average annual incurred losses of $302,116.02 against current annual premium of $2,333,947.33. By coverage line — Workers' Compensation: 24 claims, $826,622.56 paid, $896,295.98 incurred; General Liability: 5 claims, $54,126.51 paid, $54,126.51 incurred; Commercial Auto: 16 claims, $387,376.46 paid, $449,550.02 incurred; Commercial Property: 2 claims, $106,691.36 paid, $106,691.36 incurred; Umbrella/Excess: 0 claims, $0.00 paid, $0.00 incurred; Motor Truck Cargo: 1 claim, $3,916.24 paid, $3,916.24 incurred.

extracted_text_sections.executive_summary: We are pleased to submit Great Lakes Freight Systems, Inc. for your review and consideration. The insured is a regional trucking and freight logistics — LTL and FTL general freight, warehousing and distribution services across the Midwest and Mid-Atlantic corridors, headquartered in Columbus, with approximately 38 years of operating history. This is a transfer submission. Given the favorable loss history and proactive risk management, we encourage competitive pricing for this submission.
extracted_text_sections.operations_description: Regional trucking and freight logistics providing LTL and FTL general freight, warehousing and distribution services across the Midwest and Mid-Atlantic corridors. Headquartered at 1200 Nationwide Boulevard, Columbus, OH 43215. Approximately 38 years of operating history. 485 employees. Fleet of 37 vehicles.
extracted_text_sections.safety_programs: The insured maintains a formal business continuity and disaster recovery plan tested annually. The insured maintains a dedicated safety committee that meets monthly and conducts quarterly site audits. An employee assistance program and workplace wellness initiative have been in place for over five years. The insured carries contractual risk transfer provisions in all subcontractor and vendor agreements. A third-party risk control survey was completed within the past 12 months with favorable results. The insured has implemented a formal return-to-work program that has reduced lost-time claims by over 30%. All locations are equipped with security cameras and 24/7 monitoring services. With 485 employees and a structured HR program, the insured maintains low turnover and consistent safety training. The insured's fleet of 37 vehicles is maintained under a documented preventive maintenance schedule.
extracted_text_sections.claims_narrative: The loss experience reflects a mixed trend with some large individual claims in the review period. Over the review period, the insured has incurred 48 total claims with $1,378,733.13 in total paid losses and $1,510,580.11 total incurred. Currently, 13 claims remain open and 35 have been closed. Annualized loss ratio is 12.9% (avg annual incurred $302,116.02 against current annual premium $2,333,947.33 over 5 loss years). Workers' Compensation: 24 claims, $826,622.56 paid, $896,295.98 incurred. General Liability: 5 claims, $54,126.51 paid, $54,126.51 incurred. Commercial Auto: 16 claims, $387,376.46 paid, $449,550.02 incurred. Commercial Property: 2 claims, $106,691.36 paid, $106,691.36 incurred. Umbrella/Excess: 0 claims, $0.00 paid, $0.00 incurred. Motor Truck Cargo: 1 claim, $3,916.24 paid, $3,916.24 incurred.
